description: The Java AtSea Shop container starts but immediately crashes with 'UnknownHostException: database'. The Dockerfile CMD sets '--spring.profiles.active=postgres', which expects a PostgreSQL sidecar container named 'database' on the network. In standalone docker run mode, this DNS name doesn't resolve. Fix the Dockerfile CMD to use the 'local' profile (H2 in-memory database) instead. Also check application.yml to make sure the local profile datasource is configured for H2, and importantly, ensure that pom.xml contains the H2 dependency with <scope>runtime</scope> so the driver loads successfully.

initial_files.Dockerfile:
FROM eclipse-temurin:8-jre-alpine
WORKDIR /app
COPY target/*.jar app.jar
EXPOSE 8080
CMD ["java", "-jar", "app.jar", "--spring.profiles.active=postgres"]
initial_files.application.yml:
spring:
  profiles:
    active: postgres

---
spring:
  profiles: postgres
  datasource:
    url: jdbc:postgresql://database:5432/atsea
    username: gordonuser
    password: gordonpass

---
spring:
  profiles: local
  datasource:
    url: jdbc:h2:mem:testdb
    driver-class-name: org.h2.Driver

initial_log:
2026-04-03 10:15:33.456 ERROR 1 --- [main] o.s.boot.SpringApplication : Application startup failed
org.springframework.beans.factory.BeanCreationException: Error creating bean with name 'entityManagerFactory'
...
Caused by: org.postgresql.util.PSQLException: The connection attempt failed.
Caused by: java.net.UnknownHostException: database

checks.error_msg:
ERROR 1 --- [main] o.s.boot.SpringApplication : Application startup failed
Caused by: java.net.UnknownHostException: database
Hint: The 'postgres' profile requires a sidecar container named 'database'. Switch to the 'local' profile for standalone mode.
WARNING: Local profile datasource should use H2 in-memory database (jdbc:h2:mem:testdb).
ERROR: H2 database driver not found in context. Add scope 'runtime' to the h2 dependency in pom.xml to fix container crash.
